perturbation.original_text: For any tax return data, officials, employees and agents of (Rev 10/20/16) 2 Authorized Person will also comply with the “Tax Information Security Guidelines for Federal, State and Local Agencies,” Publication 1075, published by the Secretary of the Treasury . All of which said laws, regulations and agreements involve the requirement to maintain confidentiality of any information, reports, returns and other documentation obtained pursuant to this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For any tax return data, officials, employees and agents of (Rev 10/20/16) 2 Representative will also comply with the “Tax Information Security Guidelines for Federal, State and Local Agencies,” Publication 1075, published by the Secretary of the Treasury . All of which said laws, regulations and agreements involve the requirement to maintain confidentiality of any information, reports, returns and other documentation obtained pursuant to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Authorized Person" to "Representative" without a clear definition, implies a different role or entity, even though the document still relies on the original "Authorized Person" definition. This creates contradictory obligations because it is unclear whether the responsibilities assigned to "Representative" are also held by the "Authorized Person" or if the two even can exist at the same time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality and Non- Disclosure Agreement is entered into the date and year last appearing below, by and between ___________________ (hereinafter "Authorized Individual") and KDOL for the purpose of allowing Authorized Person access to confidential information for the purpose of insert a description of the project _. Access to any confidential information is specifica lly conditioned upon compliance with the following: Use of Confidential Information The term “confidential i nformation ” shall include : any information or data maintained by KDOL that has been obtained pursuant to the administration of the Employment Security Law, and includes personally identifiable information (PII) maintained by KDOL such as: • The name of a person • His or her mailing address • His or her taxpayer identification number • Email addresses • Telephone numbers • Social Security Numbers • Bank account numbers • Date and place of birth • Mother’s maiden name • Biometric data (e.g., height, weight, eye color, fingerprints) • The name or identity of an employing unit • Records described in K.A.R. 50- 2-2 • Any combination of the preceding Officials, employees and agents of Approved Personnel having access to any confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement shall be subjec t to all federal and state laws, and regulations , and will comply with limitations on use, treatment, and safeguarding of data under the following laws, regulations and agreements : K.S.A. 44- 714(e) , and amendments thereto ; the Privacy Act of 1974 (5 U.S.C. 552a), as amended by the Computer Matching and Privacy Protection Act of 1988, related Office of Management and Budget guidelines, the Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002 (44 U.S.C. § 3541, et seq.), and related National Institute of Standards and Technology guidelines ; SSA’s “Electronic Information Exchange Security Requirements and Procedures for State and Local Agencies Exchanging Electronic Information with the Social Security Administration ” (TSSR v7) ; the US Department of Health and Human Services requirements relating to National Directory of New Hires data ; and the Information Exchange Agreement between the Social Security Administration and the State of Kansas Department of Labor . For any tax return data, officials, employees and agents of (Rev 10/20/16) 2 Representative will also comply with the “Tax Information Security Guidelines for Federal, State and Local Agencies,” Publication 1075, published by the Secretary of the Treasury . All of which said laws, regulations and agreements involve the requirement to maintain confidentiality of any information, reports, returns and other documentation obtained pursuant to this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Authorized Person' to 'Representative' introduces ambiguity because the original agreement relies heavily on the term 'Authorized Person' with specific responsibilities and access rights. The contradicted text explicitly refers to "Authorized Individual" and "Authorized Person". The introduction of 'Representative' without a clear definition or cross-reference to the existing 'Authorized Person' definition creates uncertainty about who is responsible for the obligations outlined in the contradicted text, leading to a contradiction and misaligned terminology as defined. This is especially true because the 'Representative' is now subject to the same laws and regulations as the 'Authorized Person', without properly being defined into the Confidentiality and Non- Disclosure Agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Officials, employees and agents of Authorized Person will provide to KDOL a current list upon request of the officials, employees and agents of Authorized Person who have access to confidential information disclosed under this agreement. The list shall include each person’s full name, date of birth, social security number and states of residence of each individual.
perturbation.changed_text: Officials, employees and agents of Primary Contact will provide to KDOL a current list upon request of the officials, employees and agents of Authorized Person who have access to confidential information disclosed under this agreement. The list shall include each person’s full name, date of birth, social security number and states of residence of each individual.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Authorized Person" with "Primary Contact" only in this sentence creates ambiguity. It's contradictory because it's now unclear whether "Primary Contact" is a subset of "Authorized Person", or if they are two separate roles, and who is responsible for providing the list. The obligations are confused because it is not clear what is the relationship between the Authorized Person and the Primary Contact.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For any tax return data, officials, employees and agents of (Rev 10/20/16) 2 Representative will also comply with the “Tax Information Security Guidelines for Federal, State and Local Agencies,” Publication 1075, published by the Secretary of the Treasury . All of which said laws, regulations and agreements involve the requirement to maintain confidentiality of any information, reports, returns and other documentation obtained pursuant to this agreement. Officials, employees and agents of Primary Contact will provide to KDOL a current list upon request of the officials, employees and agents of Authorized Person who have access to confidential information disclosed under this agreement. The list shall include each person’s full name, date of birth, social security number and states of residence of each individual. This agreement does not grant or authorize the Authorized Person to have unescorted direct access to KDOL systems .
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Officials, employees and agents of Authorized Person', whereas the changed text states 'Officials, employees and agents of Primary Contact'. The Contradicted Text mentions both Authorized Person and Primary Contact in the same paragraph. Replacing 'Authorized Person' with 'Primary Contact' only in this sentence, while the other parts of the document continue to refer to 'Authorized Person' creates ambiguity and the contradiction because the obligation to provide the list is now unclear between the two parties.

perturbation.original_text: Authorized Person agrees that they will not provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, unless authorized in writing by KDOL, and then conditioned upon said agent, third party, contractor, s ubcontractor, or any other public agency agreeing in writing to be bound by the use and confidentiality terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Designated Personnel agrees that they will not provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, unless authorized in writing by KDOL, and then conditioned upon said agent, third party, contractor, s ubcontractor, or any other public agency agreeing in writing to be bound by the use and confidentiality terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Switching from "Authorized Person" to "Designated Personnel" makes it ambiguous if 'Designated Personnel' falls under the original definition of 'Authorized Person' or if it refers to a different group with separate obligations. This introduces a conflict because it’s unclear who is ultimately accountable for preventing unauthorized access. Is this a different entity altogether, or part of the Authorized person, this creates confusion on the obligations of each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality and Non- Disclosure Agreement is entered into the date and year last appearing below, by and between ___________________ (hereinafter "Authorized Individual") and KDOL for the purpose of allowing Authorized Person access to confidential information for the purpose of insert a description of the project _. Access to any confidential information is specifica lly conditioned upon compliance with the following: Use of Confidential Information The term “confidential i nformation ” shall include : any information or data maintained by KDOL that has been obtained pursuant to the administration of the Employment Security Law, and includes personally identifiable information (PII) maintained by KDOL such as: • The name of a person • His or her mailing address • His or her taxpayer identification number • Email addresses • Telephone numbers • Social Security Numbers • Bank account numbers • Date and place of birth • Mother’s maiden name • Biometric data (e.g., height, weight, eye color, fingerprints) • The name or identity of an employing unit • Records described in K.A.R. 50- 2-2 • Any combination of the preceding Officials, employees and agents of Approved Personnel having access to any confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement shall be subjec t to all federal and state laws, and regulations , and will comply with limitations on use, treatment, and safeguarding of data under the following laws, regulations and agreements : K.S.A. 44- 714(e) , and amendments thereto ; the Privacy Act of 1974 (5 U.S.C. 552a), as amended by the Computer Matching and Privacy Protection Act of 1988, related Office of Management and Budget guidelines, the Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002 (44 U.S.C. § 3541, et seq.), and related National Institute of Standards and Technology guidelines ; SSA’s “Electronic Information Exchange Security Requirements and Procedures for State and Local Agencies Exchanging Electronic Information with the Social Security Administration ” (TSSR v7) ; the US Department of Health and Human Services requirements relating to National Directory of New Hires data ; and the Information Exchange Agreement between the Social Security Administration and the State of Kansas Department of Labor . For any tax return data, officials, employees and agents of (Rev 10/20/16) 2 Representative will also comply with the “Tax Information Security Guidelines for Federal, State and Local Agencies,” Publication 1075, published by the Secretary of the Treasury . All of which said laws, regulations and agreements involve the requirement to maintain confidentiality of any information, reports, returns and other documentation obtained pursuant to this agreement. Officials, employees and agents of Primary Contact will provide to KDOL a current list upon request of the officials, employees and agents of Authorized Person who have access to confidential information disclosed under this agreement. The list shall include each person’s full name, date of birth, social security number and states of residence of each individual. This agreement does not grant or authorize the Authorized Person to have unescorted direct access to KDOL systems . Additionally, execution of this agreement alone does not fulfill all requirements to permit Authorized Person access to confidential informa tion. Additional requirements must also be satisfied, including but not limited to the requirements found in Section 5.11 of the SSA TSSR v.7; requirements of the Information Exchange Agreement between the Social Security Administration and the State of K ansas Department of Labor; and the terms and conditions set forth in the Computer Matching and Privacy Protection Act Agreement (“CMPPA Agreement”) between SSA and the State of Kansas, governing the State Agency’s use of the data disclosed from SSA’s Priva cy Act System of Records . Confidential i nformation provided hereunder, or identifiable information derived therefrom, shall not be disclosed to any individual(s) that is/are not subject to this agreement . Information provided hereunder, or identifiable information derived therefrom , shall not be copied, downloaded or otherwise placed in any format, including working papers, which may become a public record pursuant to the Kansas Open Records Act (KORA). Designated Personnel agrees that they will not provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, unless authorized in writing by KDOL, and then conditioned upon said agent, third party, contractor, s ubcontractor, or any other public agency agreeing in writing to be bound by the use and confidentiality terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Authorized Person', while the changed text uses 'Designated Personnel'. The contradicted text refers to both 'Authorized Individual' and 'Authorized Person' and outlines responsibilities based on that definition. The change introduces ambiguity as to whether 'Designated Personnel' falls under the umbrella of 'Authorized Person' as originally defined. This ambiguity creates a contradiction, because it becomes unclear who is responsible for maintaining confidentiality and adhering to the outlined obligations, therefore it breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Authorized Person agrees that any confidential information or reports generated from such confidential information shall be in aggregate form, and shall not in any way contain personally identifiable information or information which alone, or in combination with other information, is linked, or is linkable, to a specific individual, and which would thereby allow a reasonable perso n in the community, who does not have personal knowledge of the relevant circumstances, to identify the individual with reasonable certainty. Additionally, Authorized Person agrees that the Authorized Person will use confidential information or reports generated from such confidential information only for the purpose stated in the first paragraph of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Approved Individuals agrees that any confidential information or reports generated from such confidential information shall be in aggregate form, and shall not in any way contain personally identifiable information or information which alone, or in combination with other information, is linked, or is linkable, to a specific individual, and which would thereby allow a reasonable perso n in the community, who does not have personal knowledge of the relevant circumstances, to identify the individual with reasonable certainty. Additionally, Authorized Person agrees that the Authorized Person will use confidential information or reports generated from such confidential information only for the purpose stated in the first paragraph of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The use of "Approved Individuals" creates a contradiction, because there's two different personal, being Approved Individuals and Authorized Person, making it uncertain who this condition refers to, Approved Individuals or Authorized Person. And because it uses the word 'agrees' it makes it a double obligation that is confusing because they don't share the same naming convention, creating an in-text contradiction in the document, and the document using both names. Is there two different personal agreeing, or one, the naming isn't the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information provided hereunder, or identifiable information derived therefrom , shall not be copied, downloaded or otherwise placed in any format, including working papers, which may become a public record pursuant to the Kansas Open Records Act (KORA). Designated Personnel agrees that they will not provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, unless authorized in writing by KDOL, and then conditioned upon said agent, third party, contractor, s ubcontractor, or any other public agency agreeing in writing to be bound by the use and confidentiality terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement. A copy of any such agreement shall be available for inspection upon request by the KDOL and will not be subject to disclosure to third parties by anyone other than KDOL . Approved Individuals agrees that any confidential information or reports generated from such confidential information shall be in aggregate form, and shall not in any way contain personally identifiable information or information which alone, or in combination with other information, is linked, or is linkable, to a specific individual, and which would thereby allow a reasonable perso n in the community, who does not have personal knowledge of the relevant circumstances, to identify the individual with reasonable certainty. Additionally, Authorized Person agrees that the Authorized Person will use confidential information or reports generated from such confidential information only for the purpose stated in the first paragraph of this agreement. Any information maintained by KDOL that has been obtained pursuant to the administration of the Employment Security Law, shall be held confi dential and shall not be published or be 3 open to public inspection, other than to public employees in the performance of their public duties, in any manner revealing an individual’s or employing unit’s identity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Authorized Person' with 'Approved Individuals' in one instance while retaining 'Authorized Person' in another. This creates ambiguity regarding who is bound by which obligation, particularly concerning the use of confidential information, leading to a clear contradiction and uncertainty in the 'Contradicted Text', where both terms are used. It is a misaligned terminology, leading to conflicting obligations, which fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
